steps: Using HA primers for the SGE region of interest, set up an 8-step annealing temperature gradient PCR in a 10 µL final volume using WT HAP1 genomic DNA as template.

Determine the annealing temperature range {Tann8} of the gradient using Primer3 melting temperature as the median Tann, e.g. if Primer3 Tm = 63 °C , then use an 8-step gradient with annealing temperatures spanning 58 °C thru 68 °C . Primer3 Tm = 63 °C Gradient PCR {Tann8} = 58 °C – 68 °C Use the following amplification protocol and Kapa HiFi to prepare a HA gradient PCR Master Mix for 8 samples: HA gradient PCR Master Mix: %s %s 2. Use the following amplification profile for the 8-step annealing temperature gradient PCR: Initial denaturation: 95 °C , 00:03:00 Cycling: Denaturation: 98 °C , 00:00:20 Annealing: 58 °C - 68 °C , 00:00:15 Extension: 72 °C , 00:02:00 Cycle: repeat a thru c 15 – 30 x Final extension: 72 °C , 00:04:00 Cooling: 4 °C ,∞ Storing: -20 °C
Run the HA PCR products on a 1.5-2% agarose gel in TBE at 120V for 30- 00:40:00 . Stain with either SYBR Safe or Ethidium Bromide and check under a transilluminator.
Determine the optimal Tm and use a 1:50 dilution of the HA PCR product as template for the next step.

pUC19 adapters are 15bp sequences homologous to linearized pUC19 plasmid ends which are added 5’ to the HA PCR primers. For HA-pUC19 amplification, use the optimal Tm as determined by the HA annealing temperature gradient PCR. Use the following amplification protocol and Kapa HiFi to prepare a HA-pUC19 PCR MasterMix for each homology arm to be cloned: HA-pUC19 PCR MasterMix: %s %s
Purify the HA-pUC19 product with 1.0x AMPure. Check purified HA-pUC19 product concentration with a Qubit High Sensitivity DNA kit.

Linearize pUC19 using EcoRI, SalI and following the NEB double digestion protocol . Add 1 µL (20 units) of each EcoRI-HF and SalI-HF enzyme to 1 µg of DNA template. Incubate at 37 °C for 15- 00:30:00 , followed by heat inactivation at 65 °C for 00:20:00 . Clean up digested plasmid and determine concentration (Qubit dsDNA High-Sensitivity). Restriction Enzyme Double Digestion: %s %s

Use the NEBuilder HiFi DNA Assembly Cloning Kit for assembly and cloning of the HA-pUC19 reaction into the pUC19 vector. In a 20 µL reaction, add 50 ng of linearized pUC19 cloning vector and 50 ng of purified HA-pUC19 PCR product (from step2). HiFi DNA Assembly Protocol: %s %s NEB recommends a total of 0.03 pmol – 0.2 pmol of DNA fragments when 1 or 2 fragments are being assembled into a vector. Efficiency of assembly decreases as the number or length of fragments increases. To calculate the number of pmol of each fragment based on fragment length and weight for optimal assembly, NEB recommend the formula below or using the tool NEBiocalculator . pmol = (weight in ng) x 1,000 / (base pairs x 650 daltons)
Transform and plate on LB + Ampicillin agar plates according to the Stellar Competent cells protocol .
Grow at 37 °C Overnight .
Select 2-5 colonies and grow Overnight in 5 mL LB with 5 µL ampicillin ( 100 mg/ml , 1000×) at 37 °C w/ shaking at 250 rpm, , .
Extract plasmid DNA using Monarch® Plasmid DNA Miniprep according to the manufacturer protocol.
Send ∼ 600 ng of plasmid DNA in 10 µL for Sanger sequence verification using standard M13F and M13R sequencing primers.

Linearize the plasmid by performing PCR with the ‘LIN5’ and ‘LIN3’ primers (that omit the region of interest) for each library to be cloned. Dilute plasmid DNA to 10 pg/ul before using as template in PCR to prevent background from template vector. [Alternatively, 1 ng of plasmid DNA can be used for PCR]. Perform a 50 µL PCR with SYBR green in a rtPCR machine with extension time long enough to account for the length of the plasmid ( 00:00:30 per kb per cycle). Let reaction plateau completely to ensure enough adequate linearized vector yield.

Perform DpnI digestion by adding 1 µL - 1.5 µL enzyme directly into the 50 µL PCR reaction and incubate at 37 °C for 1- 02:00:00 , then gel purify. A minimum of 50 ng of gel-purified linearized vector is required. Additional amplification reactions and purifications may be necessary for low-yield amplicons.
Using AMP primers, amplify and clean target SNV library according to Twist Bioscience Protocol here :

Use the NEBuilder HiFi DNA Assembly Cloning Kit for assembly and cloning of the array amplification with the linearized HA-pUC19 backbone. For a 20 µL reaction with a 7:1 insert (array) to backbone (linearized HA-pUC19) ratio, use 50 ng of linearized HA-pUC19 vector and an appropriate amount of amplified array. NEB recommends a total of 0.03 pmol - 0.2 pmol of DNA fragments when 1 or 2 fragments are being assembled into a vector. Efficiency of assembly decreases as the number or length of fragments increases. To calculate the number of pmol of each fragment based on fragment length and weight for optimal assembly, NEB recommend the formula below or using the tool NEBiocalculator .
Transform 1 µL of the assembled SNVlib plasmid into NEB 10-Beta electrocompetent cells according to the manufacturers protocol.
After 01:00:00 recovery in 1 mL outgrowth media, plate 10 µL of recovery on LB + Amp agar plate.
Add remaining 990 µL to 150 mL of LB + Amp and grow overnight at 37 °C .
Extract plasmid DNA using ZymoPure II MaxiPrep kit according to the manufacturer protocol.
Sequence verify using whole-plasmid sequencing service. The trace will be poorer quality across the mutagenic region of the plasmid library.
creator: Nahum Smith
